mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals labor market tightening and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. Lower UNRATE over the preceding 3 months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.0% as of Nov 2009 reflects post-crisis labor slack
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling 2-year windows, or negative UNRATE changes correlate with positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and economic recovery, which increases corporate earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns. A 3-month uptrend in RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 352073M as of Nov 2009 shows recovery momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS trends fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate crossing above 1.5% signals the end of emergency monetary accommodation and the beginning of tightening cycle. This threshold crossing predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices equity risk premiums upward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of Nov 2009 is near zero lower bound
regime_hint: transition from monetary easing to tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals supply-side capacity expansion and labor market tightening simultaneously. This interaction indicates late-cycle inflationary pressure that compresses real equity returns. Joint acceleration in INDPRO and decline in UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.5% month-over-month AND UNRATE declines > 0.2pp in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.44 shows recovery from trough
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 500K units signals weakening construction demand and reduced forward economic momentum. This threshold breach predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 529K as of Oct 2009 remains fragile post-crisis
regime_hint: post-crisis housing market recovery with downside risk
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 50% of instances where HOUST < 500K, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals wage-price spiral dynamics. When both series accelerate simultaneously, central banks tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations. Joint acceleration in PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 0.3% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL growth > 0.3% month-over-month sustained for 2 months
failure_condition: Joint acceleration signal produces negative 60-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation trend) turning positive signal the end of financial repression and the beginning of monetary normalization. Positive real rates compress equity risk premiums and predict negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in post-crisis recovery regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS near zero with CPIAUCSL at 216.4 implies deeply negative real rates
failure_condition: Positive real rate signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2% annualized rate signals weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate earnings growth. This deceleration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward in recovery regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12990.3B as of Q3 2009 reflects early recovery from recession
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with growth deceleration risk
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration below 2% produces negative 60-day returns in < 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
